name: GDPR
scope: EU personal data protection and special-category data exposure
source_basis: GDPR Article 4 defines personal data as information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person.
GDPR Article 9 gives heightened treatment to special categories such as health and biometric data.
recommended_for: Organizations processing EU personal data, identity documents, employee records, customer exports, health or biometric data.
remediation: Restrict access, minimize retained copies, encrypt sensitive files, validate lawful basis, and apply retention controls.

name: PCI DSS
scope: Cardholder data, sensitive authentication data, and payment environment secrets
source_basis: PCI DSS defines account data around cardholder data and sensitive authentication data.
PAN, cardholder data stores, payment processor exports, and payment secrets should be tightly scoped and protected.
recommended_for: Merchants, service providers, payment processors, billing teams, and systems storing cardholder data.
remediation: Remove unapproved cardholder data, restrict access to payment owners, encrypt retained data, rotate exposed payment keys, and validate PCI scope.

name: HIPAA
scope: Protected health information and healthcare identity context
source_basis: HHS guidance treats health information with direct identifiers as protected health information.
Safe Harbor de-identification lists identifiers such as names, geographic details, dates, phone numbers, email addresses, account numbers, certificate numbers, and biometric identifiers.
recommended_for: Healthcare providers, insurers, HR benefit teams, clinics, patient support, and systems handling medical records.
remediation: Apply PHI labeling, limit access to healthcare or benefits owners, encrypt files, validate retention, and remove unnecessary PHI exports.

name: NIST CSF
scope: Cybersecurity posture across identify, protect, detect, respond, and recover outcomes
source_basis: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 organizes cybersecurity outcomes into core functions and categories.
DSPM evidence maps especially well to asset management, data security, identity/access control, monitoring, and risk response.
recommended_for: Security teams measuring data security, access exposure, credential leakage, and operational risk posture.
remediation: Inventory exposed data, reduce broad access, protect secrets, improve monitoring, and track remediation through risk treatment.

name: ISO/IEC 27001
scope: Information security management, risk treatment, access control, asset handling, and protection of information
source_basis: ISO/IEC 27001 is an information security management system standard focused on managing information security risk.
DSPM evidence can support risk assessment and control areas such as access control, asset management, cryptography, logging, and supplier/customer information protection.
recommended_for: Organizations building ISMS evidence, information classification, access review, and risk treatment workflows.
remediation: Classify information, assign owners, reduce unnecessary access, protect sensitive repositories, and document risk treatment decisions.
